chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: If you have read about set, then you must have read the set theory

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: In which it is said that set is a collection of well-defined objects

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Now everything happens in Python

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: And if you want a data type in Python in which repeated entries cannot come

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Then you can make a set

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: I will give you an example, a very good example

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: If you want to collect the list of people whose name starts with S

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: And suppose you are going to ask different people their names

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So someone tells you Shruti, someone tells you Samar, someone tells you Shubham

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: It is possible that one of your friends tells you Shubham again

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So you don't want to add it to the list

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Because if 100 people told Shubham, then 100 Shubham will be in your list

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So you want that whatever items you are maintaining in the list should not be repeated

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: For this we can make a set

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: We will see what are the methods of set and why is it used

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Many of you must have read in mathematics that set is a collection of well-defined objects

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Well, sets are of the same kind in Python

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Now what does set mean in Python?

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Why did set come in Python?

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Sometimes we have to collect some values that we want to not repeat

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Suppose I want to ask you the employee ID of those people who have already received the gift

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Now you are told that you report those who have already received the gift

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Suppose a company wants to give Thanksgiving gift to all employees once

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: And you have to ensure that no one takes more than once

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So every employee is told to submit the ID of the person who has received the gift

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: If we solve this problem with a list, then that list will be very big and it will have repeated values

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: It is possible that one employee takes the name of 5 employees

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: And the other employee takes the name of exactly those 5 employees

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Or take the name of 3 employees out of those 5

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So what will happen if this happens?

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: The list will be very big and we will need more memory

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: We don't want this

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: That's why set data type has come in Python

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So how do we make a set?

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: If I write s is equal to curly bracket

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Suppose I write 2, 4, 2 and 6

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: And if I print this

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: You will see that s is being repeated twice

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: But the set will take it only once

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So 2, 4, 2, 6 will actually become 2, 4, 6

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: As you can see

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So set doesn't take repeated values

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So this is a set of well-defined objects

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Set is a collection of well-defined objects

chunks.title: Sets in Python

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: In a very similar fashion

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Except the fact that values cannot be repeated

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: So sets are unordered collection of data items

chunks.text: Here the word unordered is very important

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: Because it doesn't maintain order

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: You can never expect that

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: First 2 comes, then 4 comes, then 6 comes

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: There is no guarantee to maintain this order

chunks.title: Sets in Python
chunks.text: You have to remember this

text: If you have read about set, then you must have read the set theory In which it is said that set is a collection of well-defined objects Now everything happens in Python And if you want a data type in Python in which repeated entries cannot come Then you can make a set I will give you an example, a very good example If you want to collect the list of people whose name starts with S And suppose you are going to ask different people their names So someone tells you Shruti, someone tells you Samar, someone tells you Shubham It is possible that one of your friends tells you Shubham again So you don't want to add it to the list Because if 100 people told Shubham, then 100 Shubham will be in your list So you want that whatever items you are maintaining in the list should not be repeated For this we can make a set We will see what are the methods of set and why is it used Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started Many of you must have read in mathematics that set is a collection of well-defined objects Well, sets are of the same kind in Python Now what does set mean in Python? Why did set come in Python? Sometimes we have to collect some values that we want to not repeat Suppose I want to ask you the employee ID of those people who have already received the gift Now you are told that you report those who have already received the gift Suppose a company wants to give Thanksgiving gift to all employees once And you have to ensure that no one takes more than once So every employee is told to submit the ID of the person who has received the gift If we solve this problem with a list, then that list will be very big and it will have repeated values It is possible that one employee takes the name of 5 employees And the other employee takes the name of exactly those 5 employees Or take the name of 3 employees out of those 5 So what will happen if this happens? The list will be very big and we will need more memory We don't want this That's why set data type has come in Python So how do we make a set? If I write s is equal to curly bracket Suppose I write 2, 4, 2 and 6 And if I print this You will see that s is being repeated twice But the set will take it only once So 2, 4, 2, 6 will actually become 2, 4, 6 As you can see So set doesn't take repeated values So this is a set of well-defined objects Set is a collection of well-defined objects And if I talk about set here There are many methods for it You can add values to it You can do everything that you do with a list In a very similar fashion Except the fact that values cannot be repeated So sets are unordered collection of data items Here the word unordered is very important Because it doesn't maintain order You can never expect that First 2 comes, then 4 comes, then 6 comes There is no guarantee to maintain this order You have to remember this Set items are separated by commas and enclosed within curly brackets As I have done here The values of it are kept comma separated And instead of square brackets Which we use in our list Or parentheses which we use in tuple Here we use curly brackets Something like this And this tells the Python interpreter Because we are talking about set Sets are unchangeable Meaning you
